researched_category.notes: Shopify is a leading e-commerce platform that allows merchants to create online stores. Its APIs extend the platform's built-in features.

one_liner.value: Shopify is an e-commerce platform that provides REST and GraphQL APIs for programmatically managing online store operations including products, orders, customers, inventory, analytics, and more.
one_liner.notes: The APIs allow app developers to read/write data, interoperate with other systems, and add new functionality.

methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 is the primary method for public apps and custom apps created via the Partner Dashboard. API tokens (Admin API access tokens via X-Shopify-Access-Token header) are used for custom apps created within a store's admin. Storefront API uses public/private access tokens.

access.notes: Shopify Partner Program is free to join. Developers can create free development stores for testing. Custom apps can be created from any store's admin (including trial stores) without a paid plan. Some advanced API features (e.g., higher rate limits) require Shopify Plus.

summary.value: Shopify offers a comprehensive suite of public APIs: GraphQL Admin API (recommended for new apps), legacy REST Admin API, Storefront API (for buyer-facing interactions), and webhooks. These APIs cover products, orders, customers, inventory, discounts, shipping, analytics, marketing, content, and more. The API surface is very broad with hundreds of endpoints/mutations.
summary.notes: REST Admin API is legacy as of Oct 2024; new apps must use GraphQL. Some features are only available in GraphQL.

agent_interface.notes: Shopify has multiple official MCP servers: @shopify/dev-mcp (developer assistant, on GitHub under Shopify org), Storefront MCP server (for AI shopping assistants), and Customer Accounts MCP server. Community MCP also exists: @ajackus/shopify-mcp-server (70+ tools). Shopify also provides official SDKs (Node, Ruby, etc.), CLI, and Direct API Access.

rationale.value: Shopify is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public API (GraphQL and REST), OAuth 2.0 authentication, self-serve free access via the Partner Program, and multiple existing MCP servers (both official from Shopify and community-maintained). The API surface is broad, covering all major e-commerce operations. No material blockers exist.
rationale.notes: The only minor consideration is that the REST Admin API is legacy, but the GraphQL Admin API is fully supported and recommended. Some advanced features may require Shopify Plus.

s1.quote: Shopify offers a suite of APIs that allow developers to extend the platform's built-in features. These APIs allow app developers to read and write app user data, interoperate with other systems and platforms, and add new functionality to Shopify.
s1.title: About Shopify APIs

s2.quote: The Admin API lets you build apps and integrations that extend and enhance the Shopify admin.
s2.title: GraphQL Admin API reference

s3.quote: All GraphQL Admin and REST Admin APIs require a Shopify access token... You can obtain an access token either by following the OAuth authorization process or by creating a private app and using that app's API password.
s3.title: Shopify API authentication

s4.quote: OAuth 2.0 is the industry-standard protocol for authorizing or giving permissions to apps.
s4.title: Authentication and authorization

s5.quote: Include your token as a X-Shopify-Access-Token header on all API queries.
s5.title: REST Admin API reference

s6.quote: The Shopify Partner Program is a global partner community that is free to join and lets you access unlimited test stores, programs, incentives, expertise, and tools.
s6.title: About the Shopify Partner Program

s7.quote: Become a partner — $1,300,000,000 paid to partners in 2025 — Earn on apps, themes, store builds, referrals and more.
s7.title: Shopify Partner Program

s8.quote: Some API features are only available to certain Shopify plans.
s8.title: About Shopify APIs

s9.quote: The REST Admin API is a legacy API as of October 1, 2024. Starting April 1, 2025, all new public apps must be built exclusively with the GraphQL Admin API.
s9.title: REST Admin API reference

s10.quote: Calls to the GraphQL Admin API are limited based on calculated query costs... GraphQL Admin API rate limits are based on the combination of the app and store.
s10.title: Shopify API limits

s11.quote: Shopify.dev MCP server — This project implements a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that interacts with Shopify Dev.
s11.title: GitHub - Shopify/dev-mcp

s12.quote: We're excited to announce the release of the new Shopify.dev MCP Server! The MCP server gives your AI assistant access to Shopify's development resources.
s12.title: Shopify.dev MCP Server

s13.quote: Connect any AI assistant to real-time commerce data from Shopify stores with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.
s13.title: Storefront MCP

s14.quote: Complete Model Context Protocol server for Shopify Admin API - 70+ tools for store management, analytics, and automation
s14.title: @ajackus/shopify-mcp-server

s15.quote: Read/write scopes for products, orders, customers, inventory, discounts, gift cards, analytics, etc.
s15.title: Shopify API access scopes

limitations: The REST Admin API is legacy (new apps must use GraphQL).
Some advanced API features (e.g., higher rate limits) require Shopify Plus plan.
Certain API features may require partner approval or specific plan eligibility.
